description: In a hallway, Ross and Rachel stand close together, embracing. Rachel has her arms wrapped around Ross's neck. Rachel says, "Dinner with candles and wine, and then maybe going back to my place."
Rachel says, "for"
Ross tilts his head slightly, maintaining eye contact.
Rachel smiles and says, "dessert."
Ross exhales slowly and slightly nods.
Ross says, "That sounds"
Ross leans his head closer to Rachel's.
Ross says, "perfectly." He leans in, and they kiss.
Rachel pulls back from the kiss and places her hand on Ross's chest. A door creaking is audible as Ross turns his head, reaches out, and opens a nearby door.
Ross walks into an apartment where TV audio plays in the background. Joey and Chandler sit in black recliners watching TV. Ross looks at them and asks, "what's this?"
Chandler says, "You get us a couple of beers." Joey raises his hand with a thumbs-up without looking away from the TV.
Ross stands in the doorway, holding his coat, and turns his head to look back at Rachel. The scene changes to an exterior view of a tall building.
The setting changes to an eye doctor's exam room. Monica sits in an exam chair while Richard sits on a stool opposite her. Richard says, "I'm going to look into your eyes now."
Monica smiles at Richard.
Monica asks, "Really?"
Richard holds a medical instrument in his hand.
Richard says, "Yeah, that's my job."
Richard lifts the instrument toward his eye.
Richard says, "All right."
Richard says, "Look up."
Monica looks upward as Richard leans in closer with the instrument.
Richard says, "Look down. Now open your eyes and look down. That's right. Look into the light." Monica shifts her gaze downward.
Richard adjusts his position slightly.
Richard says, "Look at me." Monica shifts her gaze back to his face.
Richard holds the instrument near Monica's eye in the dimly lit room. They maintain eye contact.
Richard slowly lowers the instrument and leans back slightly.
Richard says, "Okay."
Richard turns on an overhead lamp, brightening the room.
Richard turns back to Monica and says, "Your eyes look good. Those are good eyes."
Monica smiles and says, "Good. They feel good"
Monica continues smiling at him.
Monica says, "in my head."
They look at each other in silence.
Monica says, "So"
Monica shifts her posture in the exam chair.
Richard stands up and says, "it's great to see you." Monica smiles and says, "You, too."
Richard extends his hand. Monica grasps his hand and stands up from the chair.
Richard says, "You, too."
They look at each other while holding hands.
Monica softly says, "Okay."
Monica briefly breaks eye contact, looking down before looking back up at him.
Monica says, "Goodbye."
Monica releases his hand, turns, and begins walking toward the door.
Richard calls out, "Drops."
Monica stops, turns back, and asks, "What?" Richard points to a counter and says, "Drops."
Richard walks to the counter and gathers several small bottles.
Richard holds the bottles toward Monica and says, "They're free."
Monica smiles, walks back to Richard, and takes the bottles with both hands.
Monica says, "Thanks," and looks down at the bottles in her hands.
Monica looks up at Richard and says, "So, I guess I better be going."
Richard says, "Oh, okay. Yeah. I'll see you later." Monica replies, "Thanks again."
Monica and Richard stand close together. Richard leans forward, and they begin to kiss, with faint kissing sounds audible.
They continue kissing. Richard wraps his arms around Monica, and she places her arms around his neck.

The scene changes to a TV screen displaying a black-and-white image, with loud TV audio playing. The setting changes back to the apartment. Phoebe walks past the TV, approaching Joey and Chandler in their recliners.

Phoebe gestures broadly with both hands and says, "We have got to get you lazy boys out of these chairs." Joey and Chandler simultaneously raise their hands defensively, saying, "Hey, whoa. Hey. Whoa."
Phoebe lowers her arms.
Phoebe points toward the door and says, "You should go outside and be with the three dimensional people."
Joey and Chandler lower their hands, their gazes fixed on the TV.
Joey says, "No," and remains slouched in his chair.
Joey says, "Inside good."
Chandler nods slightly.
Joey says, "Outside bad."
Phoebe stands behind them, staring at the back of their heads.
Phoebe says, "You guys are so pathetic."
The TV audio changes slightly. Phoebe turns her head toward the screen.
Phoebe gasps, points at the TV, and exclaims, "Xanadu."
Phoebe steps toward Chandler's chair and sits on the armrest, her gaze locked onto the TV screen.
Chandler, without taking his eyes off the screen, says, "She's one of us now."
The apartment door opens behind them. Ross and Rachel walk in.
Rachel smiles and says, "Hi, you guys."
Ross raises his hand in a brief wave.
Ross says, "We just want to stop by and say goodnight." Joey and Chandler simultaneously say, "Good night," keeping their eyes on the TV.
Ross and Rachel look at the three of them sitting motionless.
Ross points at them and says, "Look at that. They won't even turn their heads. All right. You guys taking off my shirt?"
Ross grabs the lapels of his jacket and starts taking it off. Joey, Chandler, and Phoebe remain transfixed by the TV.
Ross fully removes his jacket, revealing his blue shirt, and looks toward Rachel. Rachel looks on with a slight smile.
Chandler, still staring straight ahead, says, "She's lying."
Ross glances back at Chandler. The video ends.
In an apartment kitchen, Rachel and Ross walk in behind Monica, who carries a white box.
Monica walks toward Chandler and Joey, who are sitting in reclining chairs, and places the box on Chandler's lap, saying, "Stop sending food to our apartment."
Monica turns back toward Ross. Ross looks at her.
Ross asks, "Why are you all dressed up?" then Monica turns to Phoebe, who stands by the counter, as Ross continues, "You're not the only one who has a date tonight."
"What? You have a date? Who wins?" Ross asks. Monica replies, "No one," and starts walking back to the kitchen. Ross follows her, asking, "Come on, what's his name?"
"Nothing," Monica says, shaking her head.
Ross leans in closer to Monica.
Ross says, "Come on, tell me." Monica turns to him and replies, "All right, but I'm very excited about this, okay?"
Monica continues, "So you've got to promise you won't get all big brothery and"
Monica finishes, "judgmental." Ross says, "I promise. What?" Monica says, "It's Richard Burke." Ross asks, "Who's Richard Burke?"
Ross steps forward, asking, "Dr. Burke you have a date with? Why?"
Monica interrupts, stepping toward him, "Why? Why should that bother me?"
Ross holds his hands up slightly.
"I love that man," Ross says.
"He's like a brother to dad," Ross adds.
Monica looks away with a slight smile. Audience laughter is audible.
"Here we go," Monica says.
Ross replies, "What? No, I mean, I think it's great that he's, like, 50. I mean, he'll be an inspiration to men who are"
Ross finishes, "almost retired all over the world."
Monica counters, gesturing with her hands, "Well, for your information, he happens to be one of the brightest, most sophisticated, sexiest men I've"
Monica finishes, "ever been with." Ross points at her, "Dr. Burke is sexy?" Phoebe chimes in from behind, "Oh, God."
"Absolutely," Phoebe adds.
Ross bows his head and closes his eyes, crossing his arms. Audience laughter is audible.
Ross turns away. Rachel looks toward Monica.
Rachel says, "You know, Dr. Burke kissed me once."
"When?" Monica asks.
Rachel answers, gesturing with her hands, "When I was seven. I crashed my bike right out in front of his house, and to stop me from crying, he kissed"
Rachel points to her own cheek, saying, "me right here."
Phoebe, looking at Rachel, says, "Oh, you are so lucky."
The setting changes to a museum display area. Ross stands holding a cell phone to his ear.
"Australopithecus isn't supposed to be in that display," Ross says into the phone.
Ross listens to the phone, his brow furrowed.
"No," Ross says.
Ross continues listening.
"Homo." Ross says.
"Habilis was erect. Australopithecus was never fully erect," Ross says, gesturing with his free hand.
The setting changes back to the apartment. Chandler holds a food carton.
Chandler, holding chopsticks, says, "Maybe he was nervous."
The setting changes back to the museum. Ross pulls the phone away from his ear and lowers it.
Ross guides a hominid model into position near a dinosaur display. Rachel sits on a rock nearby. Ross holds a small bowl.
Ross, holding the bowl, gestures to the display and says, "I can't believe this. Look, Homo habilis hasn't even learned how to use tools yet, and they've got him here with clay"
Ross finishes, "pots. Why don't they just give him a microwave?"
Rachel looks up at Ross.
Rachel says, "Well, clearly, honey, he'd have no place to plug it in."
Ross stares at Rachel, his expression neutral. Audience laughter is audible.
"Sorry," Rachel says softly, resting her chin on her hand.
Ross turns back toward the display, looking down.
Ross looks at Rachel and says, "I'm sorry this is taking so long."
Rachel nods slightly.
Ross says, "It's longer than I expected. We will have dinner, okay?" Rachel replies, "It's fine." Ross yells off-screen, "Carl."
Ross walks out of view. Rachel remains seated among the hominid models.
Rachel turns her head toward the hominid model standing beside her.
Rachel leans closer to the hominid model, resting her head against its abdomen and touching its fur garment.
Rachel keeps her head rested on the model. The setting changes to a living room where Richard and Monica sit on a sofa.
Richard holds a small photo case. Monica points to it, asking, "Wow. Is that Michelle?" Richard answers, "Yep."
"I've not seen her since high school graduation," Monica says, smiling.
Monica looks at the photo and smiles.
"Oh, my God. That night she got so drunk?" Monica asks, looking at Richard.
Richard looks back at Monica. Monica's smile fades.
"Emotional," Monica says, her volume dropping.
Richard looks away from Monica.
"She's having another baby," Richard says.
Monica says, "I thought you just had..." Richard interrupts, "no, no."
Richard points at the photo and says, "Henry's almost two and he's talking, and"
Richard continues looking at the photo. Monica holds a glass of wine and watches him.
Richard looks at Monica, "you know the other day, he told me he liked me better than his other grandpa."
Richard looks back down, "Now, in all fairness, his other grandpa is a drunk, but still"
Monica stares at Richard, her expression serious. Audience laughter is audible.
"whoa," Monica says softly.
"You're a grandpa?" Monica asks.
Richard looks at Monica, closing the photo case.
"Yeah," Richard says quietly.
Monica looks down with a small smile.
In an apartment living room, Monica, wearing a black sleeveless dress, and Richard, wearing a dark suit jacket, sit close together on a white sofa. Richard leans toward Monica.
Richard says, "Are we nuts here?"
Monica shakes her head slightly.
Monica says, "I don't know."
Monica tilts her head and smiles, saying, "Maybe. I mean, I'm dating a man whose pool I once peed in."
Richard looks down, then back at her.
Richard says, "I didn't need to know that."
Monica lowers her gaze.
Monica says, "I guess 21 years is a lot."
Richard turns his head slightly.
Richard says, "I mean, hell, I'm a whole person who can drink older than you."
Monica smiles and maintains eye contact with Richard.
"Yeah," Monica says softly.
Richard nods slowly, keeping his gaze on Monica.
Monica breaks eye contact, looking down and shifting her posture before looking back at him.
Monica says, "So maybe we should just yeah," her voice trailing off.
Richard averts his gaze.
"maybe," Richard says quietly.
Richard picks up a small bottle and glass from the coffee table, stands up, and walks past Monica.
Monica stands, saying, "Wow," and exhales audibly.
The view shifts as Richard walks toward the kitchen area in the background. Monica turns to face him.
Monica says, "This really sucks." Richard stops, holding his hands out, and replies, "Yeah, it sure does."
Richard sets the items on a counter. Monica walks quickly to him and wraps her arms around his neck.
Richard embraces Monica closely in the kitchen area.
Richard kisses Monica on the cheek and jawline, then they kiss on the lips.
They continue kissing, holding each other tightly.
Monica pulls back slightly, her hands resting behind his neck. She says, "Well, we don't really have to decide anything right now, do we?"
Richard maintains his hold on her.
"There's no rush or anything," Richard says.
They resume kissing. A muffled off-screen voice calls out, "Hey, pizza delivery," overlapping with a loud knock.
Richard turns his head toward the noise and says, "Oh, I'm going to kill those guys."
Monica and Richard both look toward the off-screen door.

The setting changes to a dimly lit museum exhibit featuring artificial rocks and foliage. Rachel, wearing a dark jacket over a black top, sits inside the exhibit. Ross, in a dark suit jacket, walks to the outer railing and says, "Rach." Rachel looks up and says, "Oh."
Ross exhales.
"I'm done," Ross says.
Rachel stands up from the rock.
Rachel steps toward the railing and says, "Yeah, well, you know what?"
Rachel continues, "So is sorrentino's okay?" Ross rubs his forehead and replies, "I'm sorry. Why don't we find someplace else?" Rachel shakes her head and says, "No."
"You know what? It's late," Rachel says.
Rachel says, "Everything's going to be closed. Why don't we just do it another night?"
Ross shakes his head and replies, "No, we won't. We won't. Come on."
Ross climbs over the railing into the exhibit.
Ross picks up a dark animal skin from the ground. Rachel gestures to an early hominid figure beside her and asks, "Okay. That's dead, right?"
The setting changes to a planetarium auditorium. Ross walks down an aisle carrying his jacket and two small cartons, with Rachel following behind him.
Rachel asks, "What are you doing?" Ross stops at a presentation console, holds up the cartons, and asks, "Do you want Cran Apple or Cran Grape?"
"Grape," Rachel replies.
Ross hands her a carton, then shakes out his jacket and lays it flat on the floor in front of the console.
"Okay," Ross says.
Ross gestures to the jacket and says, "Now sit."
Rachel lowers herself to sit on the jacket while Ross stands at the console.
"Okay," Ross says, pressing a button on the console.
The auditorium lights dim.
Ross presses another button, illuminating the dome ceiling with star projections. "Hey," Rachel says.
Classical music begins to play. Rachel looks up at the ceiling.
A loud recorded voice begins speaking over the speakers, saying, "Billions of years ago..." Ross quickly presses multiple buttons on the console.
Ross presses a final switch, and the music and voice abruptly stop. He steps down from the console and joins Rachel on the floor.
Ross lies down beside Rachel.
Rachel turns her head toward Ross and asks, "so what are we looking at?"
Ross points up at the ceiling and says, "Well, you see that little cluster of stars"
"next to the big one?" Ross continues.
"That is Ursa Major," Ross says.
Rachel tilts her head slightly.
"Really?" Rachel asks. "I have no idea," Ross answers.
"Could be," Ross adds.
Rachel rests her head on Ross's shoulder. They both gaze upward.
"Listen, I'm sorry I had to work tonight," Ross says.
Rachel maintains her position on his shoulder.
"That's okay," Rachel replies softly.
Rachel turns her head to look directly at Ross.
"You're worth the wait," Rachel says.
Ross looks back into Rachel's eyes.
"And I don't just mean tonight," Rachel adds.
Ross pulls Rachel closer, and they lean toward each other.
They kiss as a song with male vocals begins playing, singing, "I never dreamed that I need somebody like you."
Rachel pulls back from the kiss and says softly, "Whoa."
Rachel places her hand on the side of Ross's face.
"You're not laughing," Ross says quietly.
Rachel keeps her gaze steady on him.
Rachel says, "This time it's not so funny." They lean in to kiss again while the background song continues, "Somebody like, you"
They hold each other and continue kissing on the floor.
The male vocalist sings, "know," as Ross and Rachel remain in an embrace.
They shift slightly on the floor, still kissing, until the segment ends.
In a dimly lit room with blue starry lights projected on the walls, Ross and Rachel kneel facing each other, kissing. A vocal background song plays.
Rachel pulls away and unfastens a headset microphone from Ross's head while he helps pull it off.
Ross, still removing the headset, says, "some of that listen, not," while Rachel continues to assist.
Ross tosses the headset aside. They smile at each other and resume kissing, wrapping their arms around each other.
Still kissing, Ross pushes Rachel backward so they lie on the floor, the blue lights dotting his shirt.
The background song vocalist sings, "wicked gameplay." Ross rolls off Rachel and onto his back on the floor beside her.
Rachel sits up slightly, resting her hands on Ross's chest.
Rachel holds his face and says, "Oh, good,"
Ross lies on his back looking up at Rachel as she gently strokes his face.
Rachel continues to stroke his face and says, "honey. Oh, that's okay."
Ross looks up at her with a confused expression.
Ross asks, "What?" his gaze still on Rachel.
Rachel lowers her gaze, looking toward the floor next to them.
Rachel says, "Oh, no," her gaze directed downward.
Rachel points downward and says, "You just rolled over the juice box." She reaches down and lifts a crushed juice box into view.
Holding the crushed juice box, Rachel looks at Ross. They lock eyes, pause, and then both begin to laugh.
They continue laughing together. Ross places his hands on her shoulders and pulls her down into another kiss, both still smiling.
Ross rolls back on top of Rachel as they continue kissing on the floor.
The scene transitions to a projected image of a starry night sky. The background song continues.
The starry night sky display slowly pans across the view.
The background song vocalist sings, "You with," as the scene fades to a museum exhibit displaying a caveman mannequin.
Ross and Rachel lie asleep on the exhibit floor. They are covered with a large fur blanket; Ross's chest and Rachel's shoulders are bare.
They continue sleeping under the fur blanket, with Ross's arm draped over Rachel as the view gradually zooms in on them.
Ross opens his eyes, lifts his head, and kisses Rachel's shoulder. Rachel smiles and opens her eyes.
The background song vocalist sings, "you're," as the music fades out. Rachel turns her head to look at Ross, and they share a brief kiss.
They pull back from the kiss, gazing at each other.
Rachel says, "Can't believe I'm waking up next to you." Ross replies, "I know."
Ross says, "It is pretty unbelievable," smiling at Rachel before shifting his gaze upward and freezing.
Outside the exhibit glass, a group of schoolchildren in uniforms, accompanied by a priest and a nun, stare at the couple.
Ross looks back down at Rachel with a tense expression and says, "We're not alone"
Rachel's eyes widen as she looks toward the glass, and she quickly pulls the fur blanket up to cover her chest. The children continue to stare.
Ross and Rachel huddle defensively under the fur blanket in the exhibit.
The scene fades to black.
The setting changes to an apartment living room. Joey and Chandler sit side-by-side in black recliner chairs, watching a television that displays an animated cartoon. Both men are laughing.
Joey laughs, and says, "there."
Chandler leans his head back slightly and laughs, followed by Joey laughing again.
Chandler says, "Yeah," keeping his gaze fixed on the television.
A loud, rhythmic fire alarm begins blaring in the background. Chandler and Joey remain motionless in their recliners, their attention fixed on the TV.
Joey asks, "Is that the fire alarm?" without looking away from the screen.
Chandler remains still, watching the television as the alarm blares.
Chandler replies, "Yeah," without turning his head.
While the alarm continues to sound, Joey leans over the right side of his chair and presses his hand against the floor.
Joey sits back up in his recliner, returns his gaze to the TV, and says, "that's not warm yet. We still have time."
Chandler and Joey continue watching the screen calmly.
Chandler says, "Cool," staring straight ahead.
Animated characters on the television say, "Yeah, cool." The television screen fills the view, displaying the characters.
The television screen displays the animated characters standing in a hallway. The fire alarm continues to sound steadily in the background.
The television screen fades to black, concluding the scene.
